title: [BUG] Default config has only 5 entities
author: simonszu
body:
**Describe the bug**
The Readme suggests that the bedside lamp with this firmware should expose lots of sensors and states. My lamp only has 5 entities shown in Home Assistant. (The light entity, a sensor describing the light state, sensors for RX and TX, and a sensor for Uptime).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Flash the lamp with the default settings
2. Connect it to home assistant

**Expected behavior**
I expected to have, for example, sensors for the touch bar, or some binary sensors.

title: [BUG] - ota requires a platform key but it was not specified
author: snrubthinks
body:
When upgrading to blsamp2 2025.1.0 combined with ESPHome 2025.2.0 using an existing config, the following error is given when installing the firmware:

```
INFO ESPHome 2025.2.0
INFO Reading configuration /config/esphome/bslamp2.yaml...
INFO Updating https://github.com/mmakaay/esphome-xiaomi_bslamp2@release/2025.1.0
Failed config

ota.unknown: [source /data/packages/4e4ca863/packages/ota_feedback.yaml:7]
  
  'ota' requires a 'platform' key but it was not specified.
```

### Fix

As described with this [esphome PR](https://github.com/esphome/esphome/pull/6459):

Change

```
ota:
    password: <secret>
```

to

```
ota:
  - platform: esphome
    password: <secret>
```

title: Enhance Light Brightness in "Night Mode"
author: StybiPL
body:
Hello,

I have noticed that the light brightness in "night mode" is almost imperceptible on my lamp (latest revision). During regular use, a brightness level of 2% is already quite bright, which makes "night mode" appear ineffective.

It would be ideal if "night mode" brightness could be increased to about half the brightness of the 2% setting.

I understand this might not currently be adjustable in YAML configuration. Therefore, I would like to request either:

- A feature allowing customization of the "night mode" brightness directly in YAML.
- A detailed guide or instructions on how to modify the source code to achieve this adjustment.

Thank you for considering this enhancement request!
